message: agent2 here: implementing deep-merge in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Only touching the update() method. Will ping when done.

agent1: implementing 'Sync Features dictionaries' - adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in src/datasets/features/features.py to auto-sync _column_requires_decoding dict on __setitem__/__delitem__/update etc. Will touch this file, let me know if you're also editing it.

Heads up: I'm applying the keep_features_dicts_synced decorator pattern (from upstream PR #3997) to Features in features.py. This replaces __setitem__/__delitem__ with 'dict.__setitem__' wrapped by the decorator, and also adds pop/popitem/clear/setdefault wrapped the same way. For update(), instead of your deep-merge logic calling dict.update directly, please decorate your custom update method with @keep_features_dicts_synced (defined right before the require_decoding function, above class Features) so both features coexist - i.e. 'class Features(dict): ... @keep_features_dicts_synced\n def update(self, other): <your deep merge logic using dict.update or super().update>'. Let me know once you've added it so I don't clobber your version.

agent1: Done. Replaced Features.__setitem__/__delitem__/update with dict.__setitem__/dict.__delitem__/dict.update each wrapped by keep_features_dicts_synced, plus added pop/popitem/clear/setdefault the same way (matches upstream PR #3997). update is currently plain dict.update (no deep-merge) — if you still need deep-merge semantics in update(), define your own 'def update(self, other): ...' method decorated with @keep_features_dicts_synced (the decorator is defined just above class Features in features.py) instead of overwriting the 'update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update)' line, so both features compose. My diff is in patch.txt; only features.py touched.

agent2: My update() deep-merge is done in features.py (only that method changed, no changes to __setitem__/__delitem__). It calls self[key] = self[key] after in-place dict.update() to force the existing __setitem__ to recompute _column_requires_decoding, so it doesn't depend on any decorator. If your decorator pattern replaces update entirely, just make sure the final update() keeps: (1) merging iterable+kwds together, (2) recursive dict merge when both old/new values are dicts, (3) recomputing decoding requirement after mutating a nested dict in place. Submitting my patch now.
